subject: Reg CB Counter Taobao Weekly Report 20250926
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 26 Sep.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian, Jianghong,

Please find the weekly Counter Taobao project update as of 26 September
below.


[image: image.png]

Table: Counter TB Program Performance SEA + TW


[image: image.png]

Table: Counter TB Net Price Competitiveness Performance SEA + TW

Key Highlights


   -

   Aug SOB:
   -

      Across SEA+TW, SHP vs TBG ADO at 4.57x (MoM -0.09x), ADG at 1.17x
      (MoM +0.01x), ABS at 0.26x (MoM +0.01x). ABS SOB increased due to TB’s
      direct price discount (超级立减) and smart coupons in Aug. ADO and ADG SOB
      remain stable as both platforms operated with low investment
during the BAU
      month before Sep.
      -

   Overall:
   -

      Topline: Sep MTD Program ADO 123K (RR 101%) MoM +31%, ADG 1.1M (RR
      100%) MoM +19%, ADO% to CB at 6.8%, +1.6pp MoM driven by
overachievement of
      existing fashion sellers in MY (ADO 12.7K, RR 117%) and TH (ADO 13.9K, RR
      106%). Notable seller performance include Lever 3 top GGP 安阳市殷
MTD 602 ADO
      (MoM +204%) and Lever 2 top GGPs 深圳市哒 MTD 429 ADO (MoM +47%)
      -

      Target has been adjusted upwards to account for this overachievement
      of targets
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 1.8M (69% RR) due to lever 3 underachievement.


   -

   Fashion:
   -

      Topline: ADO 63.4K (104% RR), ADG $572K (96% RR), ADO% to CB 7.4%
      (MoM +1.4 pp). Overachievement is contributed by strong
performance of new
      sellers from Lever 1 and Lever 3 in MY and TH
      -

      SKU#: 1.1M (RR 80%) driven by Lever 3 underperformance (RR 44%) and
      Lever 1 underperformance in TH (RR 74%) and VN (RR 79%).
      -

      Program PC2: -0.7%
      -

   Furniture:
   -

      Topline: ADO 7.4K (RR 98%), ADG $241K (RR 99%), ADO% to CB 0.7 (MoM
      +0.0pp) mainly due to SG underachievement as top shops are under penalty
      due to order non-fulfillment rate.
      -

      SKU#: 570K (RR 175%)  MoM +73% driven by strong performance

content.question: *Lever Performance Update: *
Wishlist expansion: Review existing wishlist with local CB team and
Improve SKU sourcing rate by working with CNBI to utilise image
To adjust overall SKU sourcing target in view of overachievement.
Prioritisation of marketing resources on an expanded pool of 31
MKT and the SAI team will continue to focus on targeted pitching of

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please refer to our replies in blue below.

Slide 7

   -

   How is the F&B unlock situation in SG/MY right now? Are we doing it for
   other markets?


We have unlocked all suitable categories in SG/MY/PH and started trialing
using ERP. For the remaining markets (TH/VN/TW), we will not explore as
these markets have strict licensing requirements for F&B. Through these
unlocking, we currently have 26 F&B ADO for restricted categories (5.6% of
ERP F&B ADO).


Next step: We will explore to list more SKUs in these unlocked categories
from CNCB MP sellers (Both Lever 1 and 2).


Detailed Unlocked F&B Categories By Market:

   -

   SG:
   -

      F&B > Dairy & Eggs > Milk > UHT Milk
      -

      F&B > Convenience > Ready-to-eat > Cooked Food
      -

   MY
   -

      F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Meat Floss
      -

      F&B > Dairy & Eggs > Milk > UHT Milk
      -

      F&B > Convenience > Ready-to-eat > Cooked Food
      -

   PH:
   -

      F&B > Bakery > Breads
      -

      F&B > Bakery > Cakes & Pies
      -

      F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Meat Floss
      -

      F&B > Bakery > Pastry
      -

      F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Dried Seafood Snacks


Slide 14

   -

   Where is the contribution of sellers coming from right now? Is it JST or
   WDT?

More sellers are coming from JST (89%) vs WDT (11%).

Meanwhile, we developed a new vendor called ‘Ocean Friend’ (OF) who
onboarded in mid Sep. They can help us mass pitch, auto onboard TB sellers,
which contributed to 732 new sellers in the past 2 weeks.



   -

   What is the most effective form of acquisition right now? Via leads from
   ERP? Or 1-1? Or mass acquisition via events?

The most effective way is through the new vendor (OF), as they can mass
acquire skus and auto onboard to Shopee through system. Following which, it
is the JST events in which their business sales are actively involved with

content.question:
Slide 14

   - Where is the contribution of sellers coming from right now?

Is it JST
   or WDT?

More sellers are coming from JST (89%) vs WDT (11%).

Meanwhile, we developed a new vendor called ‘Ocean Friend’ (OF) who
onboarded in mid Sep. They can help us mass pitch, auto onboard TB sellers,
which contributed to 732 new sellers in the past 2 weeks.





What is in it for OF?
They are an ERP as well?

- Do we still face the situation where we can only take their listing
   price (and not discounted price) from ERP?

For JST and WDT, by default, we can only take their listing price without
discount. In Sep, we built the feature to let seller offer better prices.
With this, 12% of sellers did give us 10%-30% discounted price through ERP.



For OF, it was discussed that discounted prices can be offered, however, we
are currently still in the early stages of understanding their price
structure. We will pull the data and provide an update next week.



Adoption of the discount feature is quite low?

So in general, the pricing
may not be the best?

*From:* Max Goh Shi Long <max.gohsl@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Thursday, 2 October 2025 7:08 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>; Jianghong Liu <
jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Cheng
Derek <derek.cheng@shopee.com>; Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@shopee.com>;
Monica Wang <qiumeng.wang@shopee.com>; Yiyun Ma <scarlett.ma@shopee.com>;
Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <
nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Kira Tian <
kira.tian@shopee.com>; Songying Chen <songying.chen@shopee.com>; shuxuan li
<shuxuan.li@shopee.com>; Wong Si Ying <siying.wong@shopee.com>; Shi Yuting <
yuting.shi@shopee.com>; Hui Jin <hui.jin@shopee.com>; Kaiwen Zhang <
kaiwen.zhang@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: Reg CB Counter Taobao Weekly Report 20250926



Hi Ian,



Please refer to our replies in blue below.



Slide 7

   - How is the F&B unlock situation in SG/MY right now?

Are we doing it
   for other markets?

We have unlocked all suitable categories in SG/MY/PH and started trialing
using ERP. For the remaining markets (TH/VN/TW), we will not explore as
these markets have strict licensing requirements for F&B. Through these
unlocking, we currently have 26 F&B ADO for restricted categories (5.6% of
ERP F&B ADO).



Next step: We will explore to list more SKUs in these unlocked categories
from CNCB MP sellers (Both Lever 1 and 2).



Detailed Unlocked F&B Categories By Market:

   - SG:


   - F&B > Dairy & Eggs > Milk > UHT Milk
      - F&B > Convenience > Ready-to-eat > Cooked Food


   - MY


   - F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Meat Floss
      - F&B > Dairy & Eggs > Milk > UHT Milk
      - F&B > Convenience > Ready-to-eat > Cooked Food


   - PH:


   - F&B > Bakery > Breads
      - F&B > Bakery > Cakes & Pies
      - F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Meat Floss
      - F&B > Bakery > Pastry
      - F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Dried Seafood Snacks



Slide 14

   - Where is the contribution of sellers coming from right now?

Is it JST
   or WDT?

More sellers are coming from JST (89%) vs WDT (11%).

Meanwhile, we developed a new vendor called ‘Ocean Friend’ (OF) who
onboarded in mid Sep. They can help us mass pitch, auto onboard TB sellers,
which contributed to 732 new sellers in the past 2 weeks.



   - What is the most effective form of acquisition right now?

Via leads
   from ERP?
Or mass acquisition via events?

The most effective way is through the new vendor (OF), as they can mass
acquire skus and auto onboard to Shopee through system. Following which, it
is the JST events in which their business sales are actively involved with
us to introduce leads.



   - Given the momentum, are there more categories that we should start
   onboarding more?
Like HL or Furniture?

Yes, especially after we have the OF vendor in place, the sku pitching is
relatively lower effort, we have acquired 6.5k HL skus and 0.2k Furniture
skus.

We are currently still quite focused on Fashion and FB categories,
according to the wishlist from local teams, 84% of the sku needs are
fashion, but will start to explore other categories.



Slide 20

   - Furniture and F&B still quite bad?

Furniture: 82% of expensive SKUs are driven by seller margin markups, with
the majority concentrated under 润泽优, which accounts for 73% of the pool
(11K SKUs). So far, we have successfully negotiated 1.6K SKUs (15%) to meet
our target prices, while 3.8K SKUs (35%) were rejected. We are continuing
negotiations on the remaining SKUs.

   - However, 润泽优 faces structural limitations due to higher seller COGS,
   as they operate on a smaller scale on Shopee and have limited bargaining
   power with suppliers. This restricts the potential for further price
   reduction.


   - Next Step: We are identifying and engaging with direct Taobao sellers
      (e.g., 常州市希薇尔) who can offer more competitive pricing.

F&B: We are actively pitching sellers to adopt the heavy rate table in
order to address the structural logistics fee issue. ETA to conclude by 15
Oct, after CN Golden Week



   - Do we still face the situation where we can only take their listing
   price (and not discounted price) from ERP?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see replies in purple below.

Slide 14

   -

   Where is the contribution of sellers coming from right now? Is it JST or
   WDT?

More sellers are coming from JST (89%) vs WDT (11%).

Meanwhile, we developed a new vendor called ‘Ocean Friend’ (OF) who
onboarded in mid Sep. They can help us mass pitch, auto onboard TB sellers,
which contributed to 732 new sellers in the past 2 weeks.


What is in it for OF? They are an ERP as well?

OF is a dropshipper that has API integration to TB backend. We pay 1-2% fee
per order to OF.

The key value proposition of OF is that they are able to provide top TB
sellers/skus we want to source very quickly. As of today, OF sourced ~1.2
million SKUs in 2 weeks,



   -

   Do we still face the situation where we can only take their listing
   price (and not discounted price) from ERP?

For JST and WDT, by default, we can only take their listing price without
discount. In Sep, we built the feature to let seller offer better prices.
With this, 12% of sellers did give us 10%-30% discounted price through ERP.

For OF, it was discussed that discounted prices can be offered, however, we
are currently still in the early stages of understanding their price
structure. We will pull the data and provide an update next week.



Adoption of the discount feature is quite low? So in general, the pricing
may not be the best?


The adoption of the discount feature is quite low now as we are currently
pitching to only 13 sellers.

We will start pitching the discount feature to all sellers, prioritizing
those >10 ADO, and target to achieve 30% seller adoption rate. Once
adopted, we expect sellers to improve their settlement price with us by
5-10%.

They are an ERP as well?

OF is a dropshipper that has API integration to TB backend. We pay 1-2% fee
per order to OF.

The key value proposition of OF is that they are able to provide top TB
sellers/skus we want to source very quickly. As of today, OF sourced ~1.2
million SKUs in 2 weeks,



How does the model work?
They pay us comms but we pay them fee?
How is their LSR/NFR like?
Given that we have tightened DTS in CB?
Adoption of the discount feature is quite low?

So in general, the pricing
may not be the best?

The adoption of the discount feature is quite low now as we are currently
pitching to only 13 sellers.

We will start pitching the discount feature to all sellers, prioritizing
those >10 ADO, and target to achieve 30% seller adoption rate. Once
adopted, we expect sellers to improve their settlement price with us by
5-10%.



This is probably a self-fulfilling prophecy?

They will not have high ADO if
their pricing is not good?
